title:
[$250] Bullet lists render extra bullet from orphaned
and have suboptimal spacing

labels.name: External
labels.description: Added to denote the issue can be worked on by a contributor

labels.name: Daily
labels.description: KSv2

labels.name: Bug
labels.description: Something is broken. Auto assigns a BugZero manager.

body:
## Bug: HTML bullet lists render an extra bullet and have suboptimal spacing **Slack thread:** https://expensify.slack.com/archives/C090U774ZH7/p1777346806172969 ### Problem When Concierge sends HTML containing `
    /
  • ` bullet lists, two issues appear: 1. **Extra bullet:** An empty extra bullet renders at the end of the list 2. **Spacing:** The overall styling/spacing of bullet lists feels off ### Root Cause (Extra Bullet) Concierge sends HTML like: ```html
    • Item 1
    • Item 2
    • Item 3

    ``` Note the `
    ` tag inside the `
      ` but outside any `
    • `. The rendering pipeline converts `
        /
      • ` to custom `/` tags via ExpensiMark, but the orphaned `
        ` remains inside the `` container. The react-native-render-html library then renders this as an additional empty bullet. ### Relevant Code - **ExpensiMark** (in `expensify-common`): Transforms `
          /
        • ` → `/` — may need to strip orphaned `
          ` tags inside `
            ` during transformation - **`src/components/HTMLEngineProvider/BaseHTMLEngineProvider.tsx`**: Defines custom element models for `bullet-list` and `bullet-item`, and has existing whitespace filtering logic (`domVisitors.onText`) that prevents empty bullets from whitespace — but doesn't handle `
            ` tags - **`src/components/HTMLEngineProvider/HTMLRenderers/BulletItemRenderer.tsx`**: Renders individual bullet items with `CONST.DOT_SEPARATOR` (•) ### Expected Behavior - The `
            ` inside `
              ` should not create an extra bullet - Bullet list spacing should follow design recommendations ### Suggested Fix Either: 1. Strip `
              ` tags that are direct children of `
                ` during the ExpensiMark HTML-to-custom-tag transformation 2. Add a `domVisitors.onElement` handler in `BaseHTMLEngineProvider.tsx` to remove `
                ` nodes that are direct children of `` The spacing issue should be addressed with design input on the correct `marginVertical`, `paddingLeft`, and line-height values for `bullet-list` and `bullet-item`.
                Issue OwnerCurrent Issue Owner: @jmusial

                • Upwork Job ID: 2048969288184722028

                  • aimane-chnaif | Reviewer | 111254189

Observe the expense fields on the report aren't editable by userA 4. Admin changes the approver of userA to userC 5. Observe the expense fields on the report are now editable by userA 6. userA edits an expense field and hits an error. ## Expected Result: The expenses on the report shouldn't become editable after the approval workflow update. ## Actual Result: When the approval workflow is updated such that the forwardsTo now becomes the submitter's submitsTo, the front-end incorrectly allows editing fields, but the backend blocks it and returns an error. Note: Melvin seems to think this is a mismatch with logic in `getSubmitToAccountID` and `isAwaitingFirstLevelApproval` ## Workaround: N/A ## Platforms: Select the officially supported platforms where the issue was reproduced: - [x] Android: App - [x] Android: mWeb Chrome - [x] iOS: App - [x] iOS: mWeb Safari - [x] iOS: mWeb Chrome - [x] Windows: Chrome - [x] MacOS: Chrome / Safari ## Screenshots/Videos My repro: https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/5b75ec6b-f712-449d-8522-f4e5854740c6 [View all open jobs on GitHub](https://github.com/Expensify/App/issues?q=is%3Aopen+is%3Aissue+label%3A%22Help+Wanted%22)

title: [Due for payment 2026-05-05] [$250] "Switch to Expensify Classic" button on Settings page leads to 404 due to missing entryScreen

labels.name: External
labels.description: Added to denote the issue can be worked on by a contributor

labels.name: Daily
labels.description: KSv2

labels.name: Bug
labels.description: Something is broken. Auto assigns a BugZero manager.

body:
Clicking the "Switch to Expensify Classic" button in the Settings menu leads to a 404 page instead of navigating to the exit survey. **Slack thread:** https://expensify.slack.com/archives/C07NMDKEFMH/p1777307175469079 ## Root Cause `EXIT_SURVEY_REASON.entryScreens` in [`ROUTES.ts:346-349`](https://github.com/Expensify/App/blob/626a3df01a445f63a952168d9a054ab156f90302/src/ROUTES.ts#L346-L349) only permits `Settings_Troubleshoot`. However, the "Switch to Expensify Classic" button also exists on [`InitialSettingsPage.tsx:340-341`](https://github.com/Expensify/App/blob/626a3df01a445f63a952168d9a054ab156f90302/src/pages/settings/InitialSettingsPage.tsx#L340-L341), which calls `createDynamicRoute(EXIT_SURVEY_REASON.path)` without a `basePath`. On wide screens, the default central pane is `Settings_Profile` ([`SettingsSplitNavigator.tsx:40`](https://github.com/Expensify/App/blob/626a3df01a445f63a952168d9a054ab156f90302/src/libs/Navigation/AppNavigator/Navigators/SettingsSplitNavigator.tsx#L40)), so `createDynamicRoute` builds `settings/profile/exit-survey/reason`. `getStateFromPath` then resolves `Settings_Profile` as the focused route, which is not in `entryScreens`, so it logs a warning and falls through to `RNGetStateFromPath` which can't match the path → 404. The same button on [`TroubleshootPage.tsx:145`](https://github.com/Expensify/App/blob/626a3df01a445f63a952168d9a054ab156f90302/src/pages/settings/Troubleshoot/TroubleshootPage.tsx#L145) works fine because the focused route IS `Settings_Troubleshoot`. Console warning: `[getStateFromPath.ts][DynamicRoute] Focused route Settings_Profile is not allowed to access dynamic route with suffix exit-survey/reason - ""` ## Fix Add `SCREENS.SETTINGS.PROFILE.ROOT` to `EXIT_SURVEY_REASON.entryScreens` in `ROUTES.ts`. ## Related - Expensify/App#88476 — similar dynamic route issue with exit surveys, reproducible on HybridApp
Issue OwnerCurrent Issue Owner: @parasharrajat

title: [$250] Remove showRedirectToExpensifyClassicModal logic

labels.name: External
labels.description: Added to denote the issue can be worked on by a contributor

labels.name: Daily
labels.description: KSv2

labels.name: Help Wanted
labels.description: Apply this label when an issue is open to proposals by contributors

body:
## Problem Users are occasionally seeing a "Redirect to Expensify Classic" modal when clicking the **Create Expense** button from the Home page, even though they are on a group workspace and should be able to use NewDot. The `showRedirectToExpensifyClassicModal` logic was originally added to block users from creating, tracking, or submitting expenses from NewDot if: 1. They are on at least one group policy 2. None of their group policies have `isPolicyExpenseChatEnabled: true` This was relevant during the migration period, but now **all group workspaces have completed the data migration** and have `isPolicyExpenseChatEnabled: true`. The guard is no longer needed and may trigger incorrectly due to race conditions (e.g., OpenApp data not fully loaded yet). ## Solution Remove the `showRedirectToExpensifyClassicModal` logic entirely, including: - The [`useRedirectToExpensifyClassic` hook](https://github.com/Expensify/App/blob/0f49da9f6a3b391795fdb158d1b1c98623a4695e/src/pages/inbox/sidebar/FABPopoverContent/useRedirectToExpensifyClassic.ts#L37-L49) and its usage - All call sites that invoke `showRedirectToExpensifyClassicModal`, including in [`QuickCreationActionsBar`](https://github.com/Expensify/App/blob/0f49da9f6a3b391795fdb158d1b1c98623a4695e/src/components/Navigation/QuickCreationActionsBar/index.tsx#L96-L106) - The associated modal component and any related dead code **Slack thread:** https://expensify.slack.com/archives/C07NMDKEFMH/p1777061645458519
Issue OwnerCurrent Issue Owner: @abzokhattab

title: [$250] New Expensify web appears to have recreated ~238 expenses from a 2025 report into a 2026 report via repeated RequestMoney calls

labels.name: External
labels.description: Added to denote the issue can be worked on by a contributor

labels.name: Daily
labels.description: KSv2

labels.name: Needs Reproduction
labels.description: Reproducible steps needed

labels.name: Help Wanted
labels.description: Apply this label when an issue is open to proposals by contributors

labels.name: Bug
labels.description: Something is broken. Auto assigns a BugZero manager.

body:
If you haven’t already, check out our [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/Expensify/ReactNativeChat/blob/main/contributingGuides/CONTRIBUTING.md) for onboarding and email contributors@expensify.com to request to join our Slack channel! ___ **Version Number:** 9.3.59-4 **Reproducible in staging?:** Needs Reproduction (reproduction is blocked, no clear steps) **Reproducible in production?:** Needs Reproduction (reproduction is blocked, no clear steps) **If this was caught during regression testing, add the test name, ID and link from BrowserStack:** **Email or phone of affected tester (no customers):** **Logs:** https://stackoverflow.com/c/expensify/questions/4856 **Expensify/Expensify Issue URL:** **Issue reported by:** @VictoriaExpensify **Slack conversation** (hyperlinked to channel name): [#expensify_bugs](https://expensify.slack.com/archives/C049HHMV9SM/p1777267810074319) ## Action Performed: Prerequisites: User should have report for 2025 and 2026 years Logs show that within a ~2-minute window (2026-04-17 00:13–00:15 UTC), the client issued ~472 RequestMoney API calls from a single browser session. These calls originated from New Expensify (confirmed by referer: ecash, appversion: 9.3.59-4, and use of the RequestMoney command, which is not used by Classic). All requests came from the user’s authenticated session and Safari/macOS user agent. The payloads of these calls reused old expense data (merchant, amount, currency) from a 2025 report, but submitted them as entirely new expenses with fresh transaction IDs and a created date of 2026-04-16. ## Expected Result: Expenses should not be duplicated across reports, and historical expense data should not be replayed or recreated as new RequestMoney calls. ## Actual Result: ~238 new expenses were created on one report , duplicating expenses that already exist on different report. The duplicated expenses: Have incorrect dates (2026-04-16 instead of their original 2025 dates) Are missing receipt images Customer impact: The customer now has ~238 duplicate expenses on their 2026 report that need to be manually cleaned up. The original 2025 report appears unaffected. ## Workaround: Unknown ## Platforms: Select the officially supported platforms where the issue was reproduced: - [ ] Android: App - [ ] Android: mWeb Chrome - [ ] iOS: App - [ ] iOS: mWeb Safari - [ ] iOS: mWeb Chrome - [ ] Windows: Chrome - [x] MacOS: Chrome / Safari ## Screenshots/Videos For the customer details check the [OP](https://applause-ts.slack.com/archives/C049HHMV9SM/p1777267810074319) [View all open jobs on GitHub](https://github.com/Expensify/App/issues?q=is%3Aopen+is%3Aissue+label%3A%22Help+Wanted%22)
